All States · Kansas

Kansas Dietitian License

Health Occupations Credentialing. $140 application. 3 to 4 Weeks processing.

Last verified 2026-02-25 · Board-sourced data
↗ Official Board Portal (opens in a new tab)

Quick Facts at a Glance

Application Fee
$140
$140 initial application fee
Processing
3 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
Renewal
$135
Every 2 years + 15 CE hours
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Licensing Authority

Health Occupations Credentialing

Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS)
Phone
(785) 296-0061
Email
kdads.licensure@ks.gov
Mailing Address
503 S. Kansas Ave.
Topeka, KS 66603
Physical Address
503 S. Kansas Ave.
Topeka, KS 66603
Credential Categories

Kansas Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD)

The credential to practice dietetics in Kansas, regulated by the KDADS Health Occupations Credentialing division.

Temporary Licensed Dietitian

Available for applicants who have met all requirements except passing the exam. Valid for six months and can be renewed once. Temporary fee is $70.

Application Process

How to Get Licensed in Kansas

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,200 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an ACEND-accredited dietitian internship.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status is required.

Submit the Application to KDADS

Download and complete the application form. Have it notarized, and mail it with the $140 fee to KDADS in Topeka.

Provide Verification Documents

Ensure your official transcripts are sent directly from the issuing institution. Include a copy of your CDR card as proof of examination and supervised training.

Required Documents

Application Checklist

  • ✓
    Notarized Application The original downloaded application form with a notarized signature.
  • ✓
    CDR Registration Card A copy of your active CDR card to document exam and training completion.
  • ✓
    Official College Transcripts Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules
Kansas offers a Temporary License for $70 for individuals who have fulfilled the educational and supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR exam. A temporary license allows the individual to practice under supervision for six months and may be renewed one time.
Reciprocity & Endorsement
Kansas allows for licensure by reciprocity. Out-of-state applicants with a current, valid license from a state with equivalent or stricter standards, or holding active CDR registration, can apply using the standard application process to establish Kansas credentials.
Travel Dietitian Timeline
If your assignment start date is within 5 weeks, apply immediately and call the board at (785) 296-0061 to confirm current processing times.
Maintenance

Renewal Requirements

Kansas dietitian licenses renew every 2 years + 15 ce hours. Renewal fee is $135.

Frequently Asked Questions

Kansas Dietitian FAQ

How much does a Kansas dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $140. A temporary license costs $70.

When does the Kansas license need to be renewed?

Kansas regular dietitian licenses are renewed biennially (every two years). The online renewal system opens annually on January 15th for those due. The renewal fee is $135.

What are the continuing education requirements for Kansas?

Kansas requires a minimum of 15 hours of Continuing Professional Education (CPE) every two years. Holding current CDR registration (which requires 75 CPEs every 5 years) generally satisfies this requirement.
